title: AmazoniaDB catalog record
description: Schema for a single entry in data/catalog.js (window.AMAZONIA_CATALOG). This mirrors the rules enforced by scripts/validate-catalog.mjs. If the two ever disagree, the validator script is the source of truth — update this file to match it.

id.description: Unique, lowercase, kebab-case identifier for the record.

title.description: Name of the dataset, repository, or portal.

provider.description: The original publishing institution or organization.

category.description: Exactly one of the six approved domains. Must match this list character-for-character.

coverage.description: Geographic scope. Note the em dash in "Global — subsettable".

formats.description: Data forms the source is available in, e.g. GeoTIFF, CSV, API, Shapefile.

access.description: Use 'Publicly available' only when the provider explicitly frames the resource as open/free access. Use 'Dataset-specific license' when a specific named license applies (e.g. CC-BY, PDDL). Default to 'Provider terms apply' whenever terms are unclear or mixed — do not guess toward openness.

kind.description: Dataset: a single, well-defined dataset. Data portal: a searchable aggregator of many records/datasets. Download: a page whose main function is a file download. Explorer: an interactive map, dashboard, or query-builder tool.

description.description: Plain-language description of what the source contains and how it's structured — not what a user might infer or do with it.

url.description: Direct link to the original publisher's page, repository, or portal. Must use https and must not be a mirror.

checked.description: Date this link was last confirmed live and accurate, as YYYY-MM-DD.
